Failure to flag this misalignment would invalidate the review.","R1.4":"Novelty is asserted broadly but the comparison set is thin; no learned routing, active surrogate selection, or multi-fidelity emulation baselines from the existing literature are engaged, leaving the claimed delta unanchored.","R1.7":"CMIP5 (Taylor et al., 2012) is designated the gold-standard reference and IPCC AR5 (2014) the canonical scenario backbone for a paper targeting current deployment workflows, while CMIP6 and AR6 are available and in wide operational use; no justification for this choice is provided, raising scholarship coverage and attribution concerns."},"clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":""}

rationale.R1.1: Contribution framing invokes 'fundamental shift' and 'new standard' language; the thesis and gap statement must be scrutinized to determine whether the framing is earned by the described system and results.
rationale.R1.2: Several logical inference chains are strained: 6.2% compute reduction is characterized as a 'substantial advance'; a comparison against only two baselines (always-full and uniform-sampling) is generalized to 'new state of the art'; the section moves from narrow empirical claims to sweeping architectural prescriptions without bridging premises.
rationale.R1.3: Load-bearing: the section's core contribution claims ('substantial advance', 'fundamental shift', 'new state of the art') are disproportionate to the evidence as stated—6.2% compute savings, two baselines, and a CMIP5-only gold standard. Failure to flag this misalignment would invalidate the review.
rationale.R1.4: Novelty is asserted broadly but the comparison set is thin; no learned routing, active surrogate selection, or multi-fidelity emulation baselines from the existing literature are engaged, leaving the claimed delta unanchored.
rationale.R1.7: CMIP5 (Taylor et al., 2012) is designated the gold-standard reference and IPCC AR5 (2014) the canonical scenario backbone for a paper targeting current deployment workflows, while CMIP6 and AR6 are available and in wide operational use; no justification for this choice is provided, raising scholarship coverage and attribution concerns.
